actions: Established ehrlab-local graph-harness report-only control surface, provenance repair reports, evidence locator plans, and final reviews before touching processing logic.
Built and reviewed a limited read-only adapter/redaction path with hardening fixes, including recursive redaction hardening, so data-processing evidence can be summarized safely.
Reviewed existing dirty-csv scripts/docs under the ehrtopath dirty-csv artifact area and reorganized output around a patient_trajectories_grouped.csv-style temporal table.
Adjusted presentation so field groups are represented as adjacent column groups rather than overly dense single-cell blobs.
cautions: Do not export raw EHR/patient-level data, credentials, or sensitive schema into general; only sanitized project summaries may cross the profile boundary.
Session 20260426_171423_4714c0 is not part of dirtycsv; it remains the export_sanitized cross-profile boundary seed for harness-self-governance.
Treat raw sessions as forensic evidence only; normal reference should use the compiled project scope, project book, and generated graph nodes.
Future ehrlab data-processing projects should be added as explicit sibling projects unless the user asks to restore a work-tree hierarchy.

constraints.text: The export_sanitized boundary session 20260426_171423_4714c0 stays outside dirtycsv and belongs to harness-self-governance boundary memory.
Raw EHR/patient-level data and sensitive schema stay local to ehrlab and are not exported to general.

current_problems: Prior summaries were too shallow: they did not independently explain project goal, state, risks, evidence, and next read order.
Tool/skill capability nodes are useful governance evidence but can clutter the project view unless the UI can explicitly exclude them.
Project plan must become an agent-readable node so completed/todo state can update without relying on transient live-session notes.

decisions.text: dirtycsv is a standalone ehrlab project for dashboard and memory management.
Dashboard views should show dirtycsv as a project node, not collapse the ehrlab profile to a single node.
Project summary nodes must describe project purpose, actions, results, cautions, evidence, and key dependencies rather than only taxonomy.

hard_constraints.text: The export_sanitized boundary session 20260426_171423_4714c0 stays outside dirtycsv and belongs to harness-self-governance boundary memory.
Raw EHR/patient-level data and sensitive schema stay local to ehrlab and are not exported to general.

key_decisions: dirtycsv is a standalone ehrlab project, not a worktree under dataprocess.
Only sanitized summaries and project-level references may cross into general governance; raw EHR/patient-level content stays local.
Future agents should read compiled project nodes first, then evidence paths, and raw sessions only for explicit forensic verification.
Key skills/tools are part of governance evidence but must be filterable/excludable in the UI.

key_skills.name: graph-harness-maintain
key_skills.role: Project governance, provenance, boundary and graph compilation workflow.

key_skills.name: systematic-debugging
key_skills.role: Root-cause/hardening review discipline for adapter and data-processing fixes.

key_skills.name: test-driven-development
key_skills.role: Validation discipline for generated scripts, transformations, and dashboard regressions.

key_tools.name: Python scripts
key_tools.role: CSV transformation, validation, JSON graph generation, and local artifact checks.

key_tools.name: pytest
key_tools.role: Regression validation for graph/dashboard and processing helper code.

key_tools.name: local filesystem artifacts
key_tools.role: Local-only dirty CSV inputs/outputs and compiled project references.

memory_lifecycle.archive_gate: quality/scope/boundary gate before stable memory mutation
memory_lifecycle.compiled_memory: agent-readable summary/plan/graph artifacts provide long-term project context; they do not override current-turn raw intent
memory_lifecycle.live_session_ram: current active Hermes session raw messages remain live short-term context and are not replaced by compiled graph memory
memory_lifecycle.pending_update: new user facts/decisions/corrections enter here first

miss_policy.current_session_context: preserve_active_session_raw_messages; graph retriever governs historical/compiled project memory only

phase_boundaries.meaning: Build report-only graph harness, provenance repair reports, evidence locator plans, and final reviews before modifying data-processing artifacts.
Validate limited adapter/export paths and recursive redaction safeguards so project evidence can be summarized safely.
Inspect dirty-csv scripts/docs and organize the user-facing grouped temporal patient trajectory output.
Promote session knowledge into project summary, plan, decision, requirement, constraint, skill/tool, and evidence nodes used for future retrieval.

project_goal: Maintain an ehrlab-local, reusable DirtyCSV memory graph that explains what the dirty CSV data-cleaning project is, which sessions belong to it, what deliverables exist, and how future agents should continue the data transformation without rereading raw chat history.

project_identity.one_line: DirtyCSV is an ehrlab data-cleaning project that turns messy课题组CSV/field documentation into a safer temporal patient-trajectory table and supporting display documentation. The compiled project includes 19 ehrlab sessions: first building provenance/redaction/adapter safety scaffolding, then delivering the concrete dirty CSV transformation request. Outputs are local-only and must preserve patient/data boundary safeguards.

completed: Classified ehrlab sessions: 19 dirtycsv sessions included; export_sanitized boundary session excluded into harness-self-governance.
Promoted dirtycsv to first-class ehrlab project in profile/project taxonomy.
Created canonical project book, session scope ledger, and compiled-session project scope JSON.
Added summary, key skill, and key tool nodes to the dashboard/governance graph.
project_plan.source: agent-authored compiled project plan; should be updated by /plan-style project continuation workflows
todo: Keep project summary high-quality whenever new dirtycsv work lands: update goal/status/problems/decisions/requirements/constraints/evidence/read_order.
Wire future /plan outputs into plan:ehrlab:dirtycsv so completed/todo state updates as durable project memory.
Validate any future data transformation against ehrlab-local privacy and provenance boundaries.
If compiled memory does not answer a task, create a pending update/context gap rather than guessing from stale summaries.

project_status: active_compiled_memory; project taxonomy accepted; dashboard and summary quality are being hardened from user feedback; raw/sensitive data remains ehrlab-local.
purpose: Convert dirty CSV-style clinical/lab source tables and field group notes into a normalized, time-oriented patient trajectory view that downstream EHR2Path/data-processing work can reference without re-reading raw chat/session history.
read_order: project_summary:ehrlab:dirtycsv — start here for purpose, state, problems, boundaries, decisions, requirements, constraints, evidence, and plan.
plan:ehrlab:dirtycsv — read completed/todo items before proposing or continuing work.
Raw ehrlab sessions or /home/vanila/code/ehrtopath/artifacts/dirty-csv — forensic/data-local inspection only when compiled memory is insufficient and profile boundary permits.

requirements.text: When profile ehrlab is selected, the dirtycsv project must be visible as a first-class graph node.
DirtyCSV summary must include Purpose, Actions, Results, Cautions, Evidence, Key skills, and Key tools.
Preserve governance/provenance/adapter sessions as safety infrastructure for the dirtycsv project.

results: dirtycsv is now a first-class project under profile ehrlab, with 19 included sessions and one export_sanitized boundary session excluded into harness-self-governance.
The project has a clear phase split: bootstrap/provenance safety, adapter implementation/hardening, and concrete dirty CSV delivery.
The intended user-facing deliverable is a grouped temporal patient trajectory CSV plus readable supporting documentation for the field classifications.
Dashboard/project graph should show EHR Lab → DirtyCSV → DirtyCSV summary instead of collapsing the project into an ambiguous ehrlab node.
summary: DirtyCSV is an ehrlab data-cleaning project that turns messy课题组CSV/field documentation into a safer temporal patient-trajectory table and supporting display documentation. The compiled project includes 19 ehrlab sessions: first building provenance/redaction/adapter safety scaffolding, then delivering the concrete dirty CSV transformation request. Outputs are local-only and must preserve patient/data boundary safeguards.
